title: Serpyllum angustifolium
taxonomy: Serpyllum angustifolium is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint family due to the presence of many mint species. The genus Serpyllum is a group of low-growing, spreading herbs. These plants are native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. Serpyllum angustifolium is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and flowering capabilities.
